title: PipeShare BASE explorer: fix stale scope and tree cache
description:
Live debugging on OVH confirmed deploy `94e95b1` was present and `GET /api/files/tree?clientId=portal-users&jobId=8` returns 4581 files for Mike — the API was never broken. The frontend could still show an empty explorer when stale browser session data pointed at `tenant-*` or the first `portalScopes` row (`portal-users/2`, a 54MB tree) instead of shared `portal-users/8`, or when sessionStorage held an empty cached tree from a prior tenant load.

This release prefers BASE bootstrap defaults (`portal-users/8`) over tenant legacy and multi-scope drift, purges stale tenant/empty tree caches on boot, forces a fresh tree load on BASE, extends the tree fetch timeout to 120s, and skips writing oversized trees to sessionStorage.

Who is affected: PipeShare on pipeshare.live (private BASE / OVH). SaaS tenant subdomains unchanged.
changeLog: Prefer portal-users/8 bootstrap defaults when session still has tenant legacy clientId.
Skip tenant-* rows in portalScopes when resolving BASE Wasabi scope.
Purge stale empty or tenant-* explorer tree caches from sessionStorage on boot.
Force fresh /api/files/tree load on BASE startup (bypass stale 5-minute cache).
120s tree fetch timeout and no sessionStorage cache for multi-MB job trees.
